title: Analysis of Poster
wikitext:
[]: Analysis of Poster             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 20, 2013 6:16 pm SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Mar 20, 2013 6:33 pm This poster did come up before, but I can't recall when. Anyway I believe the general take on the poster was that this image represents the state of affairs after third impact begins, when all souls have been "tanged" and collected into Lilith's egg. I presume this is before Shinji makes the decision to undo everything, and these "souls" are all released. Then again, perhaps it's afterwards, and this is an allegory is straight up image of everyone "re-imagining" themselves in their old form. Does Asuka have her bandages?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 20, 2013 7:01 pm I can't tell about Asuka's arms, and searches for a Hi-res image haven't turned anything up.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Mar 20, 2013 7:24 pm I always thought those were just nondescript souls floating in the Sea of LCL. I never looked close enough at the poster to notice the specific characters. This image just got way cooler. That looks to me like the Asuka we see in Unit-02's entry plug, during the "I don't want to die x 9001" sequence. It's probably not the case, but could this be the same Quantum Rei that Shinji sees at the end, but before Asuka comes back? Maybe that Rei keeps popping in and out of existence to check up on Shinji.... I'm just spit-balling here.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Mar 20, 2013 7:28 pm Of note is the fact that I can't seem to find Shinji anywhere in here.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 20, 2013 8:50 pm BTW, there's an English transaltion of that poster over here: http://forum.evageeks.org/post/317518/Theatrical-poster-translations/#317518

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Wed Mar 20, 2013 10:18 pm This is the poster in layout form from Groundwork of Evangelion: The Movie 1 SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Mar 21, 2013 3:04 am I think it's worthy of note/no coincidence that there are all and only women in that poster. Probably a connection to Shinji's desire for a world free of pain that gets shown in a more overt light in EoE. The fact Misato is centered is rather interesting, considering in many ways she's Shinji's "mother" and if i recall correctly, she was conceived of right after Shinji for the story (referencing the "what we were trying to make here" statement) And she has a constant desire to run away from reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 21, 2013 9:26 am MandarinOrange wrote: This is the poster in layout form from Groundwork of Evangelion: The Movie 1 SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Thu Mar 21, 2013 11:01 am Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: This is an excellent scan. It makes it much easier to determine the identity of the characters. Thanks, Mandarin!

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 21, 2013 1:17 pm MandarinOrange wrote: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Thu Mar 21, 2013 2:04 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Anyone else see the GNRs in the background of the top and bottom of this image? The top one could be Kaworu, alternately.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Mar 21, 2013 3:06 pm Misato has taken the place of Rei in the series ED, but I'm not sure about the significance of that, if any.

[]:             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Thu Mar 21, 2013 4:19 pm Anyone else see the GNRs in the background of the top and bottom of this image? The top one could be Kaworu, alternately.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Mar 21, 2013 4:36 pm Güf Trüp wrote: Pretty sure its them from this picture. Can't tell if Shinji and his mom are in it though, probably not, he looks too happy.

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Thu Mar 21, 2013 4:39 pm Güf Trüp wrote: Pretty sure its them from this picture. Can't tell if Shinji and his mom are in it though, probably not, he looks too happy. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Mar 21, 2013 4:52 pm MandarinOrange wrote: Another interesting thing is, while we see Misato, Rei and part of Ibuki's faces, we do not see their eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Mar 21, 2013 5:58 pm Dream wrote: I think it's worthy of note/no coincidence that there are all and only women in that poster.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Mar 21, 2013 7:12 pm You never really get a good angle on anyone's face in here except Rei's, and it's obscured by a lot of stuff. Also, I think the reasoning behind all the characters being female in this poster is because of how the text describes all of Shinji's emotions on the poster. The English translation can be found in this thread: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/1797/End-of-Evangelion-poster/ It's a rather morbid view of women, but one that Shinji seems to have in parts of both the TV series and the film nonetheless. (It has a lot of the same vibes as this scene from the movie.)

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Thu Mar 21, 2013 11:57 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Eyes are part of the Uncanny Valley. It's possible that they were left out so that we could better identify with all of them as human.
